# SenviX All-Domain Smart Navigation: Intelligent Autonomous Navigation Cane for the Visually Impaired
**Project Lead** | March 2026 - [Fill in end date or "Present"]

**TL;DR:** Led a team to develop a hardware-software integrated smart physical cane. By leveraging SLAM mapping and YOLO visual recognition technologies, the project achieved low-latency environmental perception and dynamic obstacle avoidance for the visually impaired via edge computing on a Raspberry Pi.

## 🎯 Objective
To address the safety pain points faced by the visually impaired in complex road conditions by building a smart assistive hardware device from 0 to 1. The device is designed to recognize high-frequency obstacles ahead in real-time, construct local 3D maps, and dynamically plan safe paths.

## 💻 Tech Stack
Python, YOLO Object Detection Algorithm, SLAM Real-time Mapping & Localization, Raspberry Pi, Multi-sensor Data Fusion

## 🛠️ Core Contributions
* **System Architecture & Development Coordination:** Designed a complete hardware closed-loop system encompassing "sensor acquisition - edge processing - tactile/voice feedback," and managed the overall software and hardware development schedule.
* **Edge-Side Vision Algorithm Deployment:** Fine-tuned the YOLO algorithm for common road obstacles (e.g., stairs, suspended objects, roadblocks) and successfully deployed a lightweight version on the Raspberry Pi platform to ensure real-time edge inference.
* **Spatial Perception & Navigation Planning:** Utilized SLAM technology to fuse multi-sensor data for local map construction, providing high-precision spatial coordinate support for dynamic path planning and obstacle avoidance strategies.

## 🏆 Results & Impact
* Successfully built and deployed a physical prototype capable of independent and stable operation.
